summary: Kill 'will' to reduce future tense and make statements more direct
Probable redundancy: remove 'very' unless absolutely neccesary.
Replace 'argy-bargy' to reduce informal expressions. Meaning: (UK) a noisy disagreement[16] ranging from a verbal dispute to pushing-and-shoving or outright fighting.
Ambiguous: if referring to the punctuation mark (UK), use period (USA). If referring to the phrase, consider rephrasing to avoid confusion. Example: complete stop.
Ambiguous: replace with another word. Badly organised (UK) / untidy (UK) / determined to succeed (US)
MW linting: Consider using ---- or a div
Verbosity: recast with 'by using', ideally with a stronger verb than using.
Probable idiom: recast with 'to lose commitment'.
